issues_classified.description: UAD F1=0.725 is unanchored without a random baseline. With C(500,2)=124,750 possible pairs and 51 flagged, the baseline matters enormously. The f10_random_baseline task was explicitly SKIPPED.
issues_classified.suggestion: Compute random baseline immediately: randomly select 51 pairs from top 500 features, compute precision/recall/F1 against Chanin labels. Repeat 100 times. Report mean + std. This is computationally trivial (<1 minute) and essential for interpreting the 0.725 claim.

issues_classified.description: Multi-seed 'robustness' is determinism, not robustness. All three seeds (42, 123, 456) produce IDENTICAL results -- same 51 pairs, same 29 TPs, same supervised_labels dictionary. Standard deviation is exactly 0.000.
issues_classified.suggestion: Rename Section 4.3 to 'Multi-Seed Determinism' or 'Reproducibility Validation.' Remove all 'robustness' claims. Explicitly state: 'The perfect consistency reflects determinism on a fixed pre-trained SAE, not robustness to SAE retraining, corpus change, or model variation.'

issues_classified.description: DFDA's 99.5% MSE improvement is a metric tautology, not absorption recovery. The MLP learns to predict near-zero parent values in child-dominant positions. Compensated MSE values are 1.5e-10, 5.6e-10, 2.2e-08 -- essentially machine epsilon.
issues_classified.suggestion: Either (a) completely remove DFDA from the paper and focus solely on UAD, or (b) relegate DFDA to a single paragraph in Future Work with NO results table and NO percentage figures. Do not report 99.5% anywhere.

issues_classified.description: Cross-layer 'validation' masks a layer-dependent failure. Layer 4 achieves F1=0.432, below the 0.5 minimum threshold. Mean F1=0.561 is entirely driven by layer 8's outlier performance (F1=0.725).
issues_classified.suggestion: Report layer-wise results individually and honestly: 'UAD performs well at layer 8 (F1=0.725) but falls below threshold at layer 4 (F1=0.432), suggesting absorption signatures are strongest in mid-to-late layers where hierarchical feature structure is most pronounced.' Do not present mean F1=0.561 as a validation result.

issues_classified.description: No ablation studies were conducted. Design choices (HAC, phi coefficient, 50 clusters, top 500 features) are unjustified without testing alternatives.
issues_classified.suggestion: Run ablations: (1) k-means vs HAC, (2) raw co-occurrence vs phi coefficient, (3) 25 vs 50 vs 100 clusters, (4) top 250 vs 500 vs 1000 features. Each ablation is computationally cheap (~10 seconds). Report which design choices contribute to performance.

issues_classified.description: No statistical significance testing is reported. Bootstrap CI is defined in notation.md but noted as 'not yet implemented.' Without CIs, p-values, or bootstrap estimates, F1=0.725 has no statistical grounding.
issues_classified.suggestion: Implement bootstrap CI for F1: resample the 1000 examples with replacement 1000 times, recompute F1 each time, report 95% CI. Also compute a permutation test: randomly shuffle cluster assignments and compute F1 distribution under null. Report p-value for F1 > null.

issues_classified.description: No analysis of false positives. With precision=0.569, 22 of 51 same-cluster pairs are false positives. The paper acknowledges this but never analyzes what these false positives are.
issues_classified.suggestion: Analyze the 22 false positive pairs: report their phi coefficients, marginal activation frequencies, and any semantic patterns. Categorize them (e.g., topical co-occurrence, shared super-absorber, random). This analysis could suggest post-hoc filters to raise precision above 0.8.

issues_classified.description: The novelty claim depends on UAD being the 'first' unsupervised absorption detection method. While the literature search found no direct competitor, the core machinery -- co-occurrence clustering on phi matrices -- is not novel. 'Geometry of Concepts' uses spectral clustering on phi matrices for functional lobe discovery.
issues_classified.suggestion: Strengthen novelty by: (1) explicitly contrasting UAD's parent-child pair identification with 'Geometry of Concepts' functional lobe discovery, (2) showing via ablation that the parent-child direction assignment (low marginal = parent) is critical and non-obvious, (3) demonstrating that UAD outperforms spectral clustering on the same task.

issues_classified.description: Single model (GPT-2 Small), single SAE configuration (d_SAE=24,576 JumpReLU), single concept domain (first letters a-z), single layer (layer 8 is the only one above threshold). The paper claims 'Any SAE, any corpus' applicability in Table 1.
issues_classified.suggestion: Scope claims narrowly: 'UAD detects first-letter absorption in GPT-2 Small layer 8 without supervision.' Remove 'Any SAE, any corpus' from Table 1 until cross-model validation is completed. Add a 'Generalization Limitations' paragraph explicitly stating what has and has not been tested.

issues_classified.description: Missing E4 experiment creates structural confusion. Section 4.5 is labeled 'E5: DFDA Scaling' with no E4 anywhere. The outline also jumps from E3 to E5.
issues_classified.suggestion: Renumber experiments sequentially: E1 (UAD full), E2 (multi-seed), E3 (cross-layer), E4 (DFDA). Update all references in the paper, outline, and experiments section.

issues_classified.description: Table numbering is inconsistent between outline and paper. The outline plans Table 1=Main Results, Table 2=DFDA Detail, Table 3=Prior Work. The paper uses Table 4=DFDA Detail, Table 5=Main Results.
issues_classified.suggestion: Standardize table numbering across all documents. Use Table 1=Main UAD Results, Table 2=DFDA Detail (if retained), Table 3=Prior Work Comparison. Update all in-text references.

issues_classified.description: Figure 1 (UAD pipeline) is referenced in Section 3.3 but does not exist. Only fig1_desc.md exists; no PDF has been generated. This is a submission blocker.
issues_classified.suggestion: Generate figures/fig1.pdf as a flow diagram showing the six-step UAD pipeline. Use matplotlib or TikZ with the color palette defined in visual_audit.md (#4472C4, #ED7D31, #70AD47).

issues_classified.description: Table 4 (DFDA) shows identical phi values (0.8123201004396181) for pairs 1-4 and 6. This is suspicious -- different feature pairs should have different phi coefficients.
issues_classified.suggestion: Verify phi values in the source data (f5_dfda_scale_results.json). If correct, add a footnote explaining why multiple pairs share the same phi (e.g., they come from the same cluster with identical co-occurrence patterns). If incorrect, correct them.

issues_classified.description: The citation [Elhage et al., 2022] is used to support 'mid-to-late layers contain the most structured feature hierarchies' (Section 4.4), but Elhage et al. is about superposition, not layer-wise hierarchy structure.
issues_classified.suggestion: Find a citation specifically about layer-wise hierarchy in transformers, or soften the claim to 'consistent with the general observation that mid-to-late layers encode more abstract semantic features.'

issues_classified.description: The paper uses 'PARTIAL_PASS' terminology in Section 4.1 for P3. This is internal validation language that should not appear in the paper draft.
issues_classified.suggestion: Replace 'PARTIAL_PASS' with standard prose: 'We designated cross-layer as a partial pass and proceeded to full experiments.'

issues_classified.description: P3 appears without context explaining why cross-layer validation was tested.
issues_classified.suggestion: Add a purpose clause: 'To test whether UAD's detection signature generalizes across model layers, we evaluated layers 4, 8, and 10.'

issues_classified.description: DFDA caveat is strong but buried in paragraph 3 of Section 4.5. Readers scanning may miss it.
issues_classified.suggestion: Add a prominent preliminary marker at the start of Section 4.5: '**Preliminary Result -- Metric Under Revision.**' Move the caveat to the first paragraph.

issues_classified.description: Section 4.1 says '99.999% mean MSE improvement' but should say '99.5% mean' to match Section 4.5 and the data.

issues_fixed: Dead feature catastrophe (89-99% in Iteration 0): Fixed by switching to training-free analysis using pre-trained JumpReLU SAE
Confounded architecture comparison (JumpReLU vs TopK): Fixed by removing cross-architecture comparison entirely
H1-H4 overexpansion in paper scope: Fixed by reframing paper around UAD + DFDA, removing original hypotheses
UAD underpowered (25 collisions in Iteration 0): Fixed by scaling to 51 same-cluster pairs with 29 TPs
Abstract overclaim '11.1% MSE improvement': Removed in Iteration 1 reframing (but new '99.5%' overclaim introduced)
success_patterns: Honest DFDA caveat: Explicit disclosure that 'near-100% improvement is artifactual' builds reviewer trust
Determinism vs robustness distinction: Shows sophisticated understanding of experimental design
Precision contextualization: 'screening tool, not classifier' framing manages expectations honestly
Verifiable data integrity: Every number in every table matches source JSON files exactly
UAD genuine novelty: F1=0.725 with perfect recall, first unsupervised absorption detection method
Zero-failure experiment execution: All 6 tasks completed successfully
Successful pivot from Iteration 0: Reframed from failed cross-architecture benchmark to focused UAD method paper
Clear algorithm description: Six-step UAD pipeline is precise enough to reimplement
systemic_patterns: Missing baselines as recurring issue: Iteration 0 lacked true absorption validation; Iteration 1 lacks random baseline
Overclaiming in high-visibility locations: Iteration 0 Abstract led with 4x architecture difference; Iteration 1 Abstract reports 99.5% DFDA improvement
Structural defects persist across iterations: Iteration 0 had terminology drift and figure issues; Iteration 1 has missing E4, table numbering mismatch, missing Figure 1
Single-model validation as persistent limitation: Both iterations blocked on cross-model validation (Gemma-2B gated, Pythia SAE unavailable)
Honest negative result reporting as consistent strength: Both iterations praised for transparency about limitations

suggestions: All 6 tasks completed on single GPU with minimal idle time---excellent scheduling
Total experiment time: ~6 minutes (3 pilots: ~3 min, 3 full: ~3 min). Well within 1-hour budget
Actual runtimes (~1s per task) were far below planned times (15-30 min), indicating overestimation of computational requirements
Ablations and random baseline (100 repeats) could be parallelized if multiple GPUs available, each taking ~10 seconds

recommended_focus: Compute random baseline (critical): <1 minute, essential for interpreting F1=0.725
Remove or demote DFDA (critical): 99.5% figure damages credibility
Fix structural defects (critical): missing E4, table numbering, Figure 1, robustness reframing
Run ablations (major): k-means vs HAC, phi vs raw, cluster counts, feature counts. Each ~10 seconds
Implement statistical testing (major): bootstrap CI + permutation test
Analyze false positives (major): understand 22/51 false positives to guide post-hoc filtering
Scope claims honestly (major): remove 'Any SAE, any corpus' until cross-model validation completed
